capability_dilemma_fit.fit_rationale: SAP's ecosystem control score reflects strong leverage from the BDC-Databricks alliance and the Reltio acquisition adding MDM capability. Execution velocity is moderate, constrained by DSAG trust deficit and migration friction.

selected_dimensions.rationale: Scenario A's self-service onboarding thesis is most sensitive to ecosystem control (BDC reach) and the underlying MDM data harmonisation capability from Reltio.

actor_derivation.objective_alignment: Grow BDC adoption beyond early adopters to the broader SAP customer base
constraint_focus: Multi-year ERP migration timelines for existing S/4HANA customers
DSAG trust deficit: 83% BDC unfamiliarity rate
derivation_notes: Derive actor path from planned scene A

required: SAP must launch a migration-phase pricing reform before Reltio capabilities go GA
Reltio MDM onboarding must be exposed via a low-friction API layer compatible with existing customer data architectures
warning: If DSAG trust deficit is not addressed within 18 months, competitive framing by Salesforce and Microsoft will deepen
blocking: Mid-migration billing friction must be resolved or Reltio premium pricing will be blocked by customer perception

capability_dilemma_fit.fit_rationale: Scenario B requires high execution velocity to close a joint Databricks-Reltio integration roadmap before competitive window closes. Ecosystem control is moderate — Databricks partnership is pre-established but not deepened yet.

selected_dimensions.rationale: Scenario B's Databricks channel thesis is most sensitive to SAP's ability to move quickly on joint go-to-market.

constraint_focus: Databricks competing interests in open data formats

capability_dilemma_fit.fit_rationale: Scenario C is an adverse outcome where adoption stalls. SAP's ecosystem control weakens as DSAG voices dominate the narrative. The blocking condition — unresolved migration friction — is the primary risk driver.

selected_dimensions.rationale: Scenario C's stall dynamic is most sensitive to whether SAP can demonstrate migration simplification to existing BDC customers within 6 months.

actor_derivation.objective_alignment: Reduce customer migration friction and eliminate mid-migration billing pain
constraint_focus: DSAG trust deficit: 83% BDC unfamiliarity rate
Enterprise data gravity — customers cannot migrate all data to SAP systems

warning: If SAP does not address mid-migration billing friction within 6 months, Scenario C's stall dynamic becomes difficult to reverse
blocking: Without a customer-facing simplification narrative and pricing reform, negative DSAG perception blocks BDC pipeline growth

evidence_refs.summary: DSAG perception blocks BDC growth without simplification narrative

Scene constraints interpreted: DSAG trust deficit, enterprise data gravity

A_vs_B.rationale: Scenario A has stronger ecosystem control alignment and a clearer path to execution. Scenario B requires an unconfirmed Databricks commitment that is not yet public.

A_vs_C.rationale: Scenario C is an adverse outcome dependent on SAP failing to address known migration friction. Scenario A requires action but has a path to resolution.

B_vs_C.rationale: Scenario B is a conditional upside (requires Sapphire announcement). Scenario C is a structural downside risk. Both require proactive intervention.

recommendation_summary: Scenario A (Reltio MDM as BDC Onboarding Accelerator) is the highest-confidence path, contingent on SAP addressing migration-phase pricing friction before Reltio capabilities reach GA. Scenario B offers incremental upside via the Databricks channel if a joint announcement is made at Sapphire 2026. Scenario C is the dominant risk path and should be treated as the primary downside scenario to monitor.
